title: orobanche parishii subsp. parishii
intro: Orobanche parishii subsp. parishii, also known as the Indian broomrape or Orobanche parishii, is a parasitic flowering plant that derives its nutrients by attaching itself to the roots of its host plant, typically those from the Fabaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Orobanche parishii subsp. parishii include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, sore throat, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and act as an anti-cancer agent. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to offer therapeutic benefits when consumed or applied topically.
